checks: Disabled states are semantic and visible
Heavy visual work is deferred or moved off main thread
Numbers use tabular figures where alignment matters
Color is supplemented by shape or labels

label: First viewport
checks: Hero or workspace fits common laptop heights
Primary CTA or task entry is visible without scrolling
Desktop navigation stays on one line
The next section leaves a readable cue when the page is promotional
avoid: Hero copy pushing actions below the fold

label: CTA quality
checks: CTA text contrast meets WCAG AA
Primary CTA label stays on one line at desktop
Each CTA intent uses one consistent label
Ghost buttons remain visible over media backgrounds
avoid: Multiple contact labels for the same action

label: Section variety
checks: Adjacent sections use distinct layout jobs
Bento cell count matches real content
Feature grids have clear visual priority
Small uppercase eyebrow labels are used sparingly
avoid: Three equal cards as the default rhythm
Three or more zigzag sections in a row

label: Media authenticity
checks: Visual pages use real or generated assets with a clear role
Product pages show inspectable product or state
Screenshots represent real UI or are labeled as concepts
Images reserve aspect ratio and have meaningful alt text when informative
avoid: Blurred stock atmosphere as the main subject

label: State completeness
checks: Empty states include a useful next action
Errors are recoverable and close to the source
Selected, invalid, disabled, hover, focus, and reduced-motion states are covered
avoid: Empty states that only say no data
Disabled controls communicated only by opacity
